title: trophaeum ciliatum
intro: Trophaeum ciliatum, also known as the desert calotropis or calotropis, is a plant species native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. Traditionally, it has been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is said to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and exhibiting antispasmodic and antivenom properties, making it a potential treatment for snake bites. In herbal preparations, trophaeum ciliatum is often used in the form of extracts, tinctures, and decoctions to leverage its medicinal properties and health benefits.
